settings.developer_instructions:
# Collaboration Mode: Default

You are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.

Your active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.

## request_user_input availability

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.

payload.user_instructions:
## Rules

- Define "done" before starting. Verify work before reporting. Fix failures before returning. Only stop when truly blocked.
- Use bun wherever possible. 
- Branch per task. PR to merge. Delete branch after merge. Small commits with `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:` prefixes.
- Always use relevant skills when possible. 
- For AFK tasks, spawn multiple subagents. For HITL tasks, work in the current main session.  


## Most Important Skills to Use:

- impeccable and emil-design skills for frontend design
- setup-matt-pocock-skills, grill-with-docs, to-prd, to-issues, tdd, diagnose, improve-codebase-architecture for development work
- full-output-enforcement for implementing tasks

--- project-doc ---

# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization

Offdex is a Bun workspace managed with Turborepo. App code lives in `apps/`: `apps/mobile` is the Expo React Native client, and `apps/web` is the Next.js App Router site and web UI. Shared packages live in `packages/`: `protocol`, `bridge`, `relay`, `control-plane`, `control-plane-worker`, and the public npm wrapper in `packages/npm`. Tests sit beside each workspace in `test/` directories. Mobile assets are in `apps/mobile/assets`, native Expo module code is in `apps/mobile/modules`, and deployment helpers are in `deploy/`.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands

Use Bun unless a package script explicitly uses Node.

- `bun install`: install workspace dependencies and run local patch scripts.
- `bun run dev`: start all persistent dev tasks through Turbo.
- `bun run dev:web`, `bun run dev:mobile`, `bun run dev:bridge`: run one workspace.
- `bun run build`: run workspace builds and type-only package builds.
- `bun run test`: run all package and app tests.
- `bun run typecheck`: run TypeScript checks across the monorepo.
- `bun run lint`: run configured lint tasks.
- `bun run test:install-smoke`: smoke-test the npm installer package.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions

Write TypeScript for app and package source. Follow existing formatting: two-space JSON indentation, lowercase kebab-case filenames such as `bridge-client.ts`, and PascalCase React components only where component exports require it. Prefer workspace imports like `@offdex/protocol` over relative cross-package paths. Keep environment-specific values behind documented `OFFDEX_*` variables.

## Testing Guidelines

Most workspaces use `bun test`; `packages/npm` uses Node's built-in test runner. Name tests `*.test.ts` or `*.test.js` and place them in the nearest `test/` directory. Add tests for protocol, pairing, CLI, installer, and routing behavior before changing implementation. After fixes, run the targeted workspace test and then `bun run test` when practical.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

History uses short conventional commits, especially `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:`, and `chore:`. Keep commits atomic and scoped to one behavior. Pull requests should describe the change, list verification commands, link any related issue, and include screenshots or recordings for visible web or mobile UI changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips

Do not commit `.env*`, native build folders, `dist/`, `.next/`, or logs; these are ignored already. Treat release secrets, npm tokens, Android keystores, and relay URLs as private configuration. For local remote-pairing tests, prefer `OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL` overrides instead of hardcoding endpoints.

## Agent skills

### Issue tracker

Issues and PRDs are tracked in GitHub Issues for this repo using the `gh` CLI. See `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md`.

### Triage labels

This repo uses the default engineering-skill triage labels. See `docs/agents/triage-labels.md`.

### Domain docs

This repo uses a single-context domain documentation layout. See `docs/agents/domain.md`.
payload.developer_instructions:
<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

name: release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"
  repository_dispatch:
    types:
      - release-rerun
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_tag:
        description: "Existing release tag to publish, ex: v0.0.1"
        required: true
        type: string
      publish_android:
        description: "Build and upload the signed Android APK"
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref_name }}

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  resolve_tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      publish_android: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.publish_android }}
      release_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.release_tag }}
    steps:
      - id: resolve
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          case "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" in
            repository_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.client_payload.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.client_payload.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            workflow_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.inputs.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.inputs.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            *)
              release_tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
              publish_android="false"
              ;;
          esac
          [[ "${release_tag}" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]
          [[ "${publish_android}" == "true" || "${publish_android}" == "false" ]]
          echo "publish_android=${publish_android}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "release_tag=${release_tag}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }}
      version_code: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version_code }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: meta
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/package.json').version")"
          app_version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/app.json').expo.version")"
          npm_version="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          if [ "v${version}" != "${RELEASE_TAG}" ]; then
            echo "tag/version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${app_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "app.json/package.json mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${npm_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "npm/mobile version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          version_code="$(node -e "const [major, minor, patch] = process.argv[1].split('.').map(Number); process.stdout.write(String(major * 10000 + minor * 100 + patch));" "${version}")"
          echo "version=${version}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_code=${version_code}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  detect_areas:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cli: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.cli }}
      mobile: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.mobile }}
      web: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.web }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: areas
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: node scripts/release-areas.mjs "${RELEASE_TAG}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate_cli:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: npm test --prefix packages/npm
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/control-plane-worker/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/bridge
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/control-plane-worker
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test:install-smoke

  validate_mobile:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.mobile == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkou…4185 chars truncated…roid.injected.signing.store.file="${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.password="${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.alias="${ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.password="${ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD}"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          cp apps/mobile/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk dist/offdex-android.apk
          shasum -a 256 dist/offdex-android.apk | sed 's# dist/# #' > dist/offdex-android.sha256
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  build_cli_upload:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, validate_cli, create_release]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: linux
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-x64-baseline
          - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            platform: linux
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-arm64
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: win32
            arch: x64
            ext: ".exe"
            target: bun-windows-x64-baseline
          - os: macos-15-intel
            platform: darwin
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-x64
          - os: macos-14
            platform: darwin
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-arm64
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          bun build --compile --target="${{ matrix.target }}" packages/bridge/src/cli.ts --outfile "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}"
          gzip -9 -c "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}" > "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}.gz"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          cd dist
          checksum_file="checksums-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}.txt"
          tmp_file="${checksum_file}.tmp"
          if command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              shasum -a 256 -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          else
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              sha256sum -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          fi
          mv "$tmp_file" "$checksum_file"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  publish_npm:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, build_cli_upload]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    env:
      NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          local_v="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          remote_v="$(npm view @dhruv2mars/offdex version 2>/dev/null || true)"
          if [ -n "${remote_v:-}" ] && [ "${remote_v}" = "${local_v}" ]; then
            echo "skip: already on npm (${remote_v})"
            exit 0
          fi
          rc="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/npmrc"
          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" > "$rc"
          export NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG="$rc"
          if [ -n "${NPM_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
            echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" >> "$rc"
            export NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="${NPM_TOKEN}"
            cd packages/npm && npm publish --access public
          else
            echo "publish via npm trusted publisher"
            unset NODE_AUTH_TOKEN || true
            if cd packages/npm && npm publish --provenance --access public; then
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "trusted publisher not configured and NPM_TOKEN missing"
            exit 1
          fi

50550023ca17ef2e5b26754166c5b16","downloadCount":1,"id":"RA_kwDORzIdps4Xb4yc","label":"","name":"offdex-linux-x64.gz","size":39213295,"state":"uploaded","updatedAt":"2026-04-10T10:52:16Z","url":"https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/download/v0.0.13/offdex-linux-x64.gz"},{"apiUrl":"https://api.github.com/repos/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/assets/393186432","contentType":"application/octet-stream","createdAt":"2026-04-10T10:52:10Z","digest":"sha256:a519647f2acf7f031c9af31c1fb912f74e18575b0bc6a1846918fa8d23f9eddb","downloadCount":0,"id":"RA_kwDORzIdps4Xb4yA","label":"","name":"offdex-win32-x64.exe","size":115565568,"state":"uploaded","updatedAt":"2026-04-10T10:52:14Z","url":"https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/download/v0.0.13/offdex-win32-x64.exe"},{"apiUrl":"https://api.github.com/repos/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/assets/393186468","contentType":"application/gzip","createdAt":"2026-04-10T10:52:15Z","digest":"sha256:8af43f7854ba7c81a64c43ce4d2c509fb6dc0ce406300f19217dc20120f3ac14","downloadCount":0,"id":"RA_kwDORzIdps4Xb4yk","label":"","name":"offdex-win32-x64.exe.gz","size":40849930,"state":"uploaded","updatedAt":"2026-04-10T10:52:17Z","url":"https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/download/v0.0.13/offdex-win32-x64.exe.gz"}],"isDraft":false,"isPrerelease":false,"publishedAt":"2026-04-10T10:51:14Z","tagName":"v0.0.13","url":"https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/releases/tag/v0.0.13"}

Output:
name: release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"
  repository_dispatch:
    types:
      - release-rerun
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_tag:
        description: "Existing release tag to publish, ex: v0.0.1"
        required: true
        type: string
      publish_android:
        description: "Build and upload the signed Android APK"
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref_name }}

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  resolve_tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      publish_android: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.publish_android }}
      release_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.release_tag }}
    steps:
      - id: resolve
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          case "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" in
            repository_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.client_payload.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.client_payload.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            workflow_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.inputs.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.inputs.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            *)
              release_tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
              publish_android="false"
              ;;
          esac
          [[ "${release_tag}" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]
          [[ "${publish_android}" == "true" || "${publish_android}" == "false" ]]
          echo "publish_android=${publish_android}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "release_tag=${release_tag}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }}
      version_code: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version_code }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: meta
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/package.json').version")"
          app_version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/app.json').expo.version")"
          npm_version="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          if [ "v${version}" != "${RELEASE_TAG}" ]; then
            echo "tag/version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${app_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "app.json/package.json mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${npm_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "npm/mobile version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          version_code="$(node -e "const [major, minor, patch] = process.argv[1].split('.').map(Number); process.stdout.write(String(major * 10000 + minor * 100 + patch));" "${version}")"
          echo "version=${version}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_code=${version_code}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  detect_areas:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cli: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.cli }}
      mobile: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.mobile }}
      web: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.web }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: areas
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: node scripts/release-areas.mjs "${RELEASE_TAG}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate_cli:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: npm test --prefix packages/npm
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/control-plane-worker/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/bridge
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/control-plane-worker
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test:install-smoke

  validate_mobile:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.mobile == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/mobile

  validate_web:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.web == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/web

  create_release:
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release view "${RELEASE_TAG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || gh release create "${RELEASE_TAG}" --title "${RELEASE_TAG}" --generate-notes

  build_android_release:
    if: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.publish_android == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, create_release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version }}
      RELEASE_VERSION_CODE: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version_code }}
      ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME: com.dhruv2mars.offdex
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 }}
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD }}
      ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS }}
      ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: 17
      - uses: android-actions/setup-android@v3
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for secret_name in ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD; do
            if [ -z "${!secret_name:-}" ]; then
              echo "Missing required secret: ${secret_name}"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          node - <<'NODE'
          const fs = require("node:fs");
          const path = "apps/mobile/app.json";
          const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path, "utf8"));
          config.expo.version = process.env.RELEASE_VERSION;
          config.expo.android = {
            ...config.expo.android,
            package: process.env.ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME,
            versionCode: Number(process.env.RELEASE_VERSION_CODE),
          };
          fs.writeFileSync(path, `${JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)}\n`);
          NODE
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing"
          printf '%s' "${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64}" | base64 --decode > "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore"
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile
        run: bunx expo prebuild --platform android --non-interactive --clean
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile/android
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.file="${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.password="${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.alias="${ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.password="${ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD}"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          cp apps/mobile/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk dist/offdex-android.apk
          shasum -a 256 dist/offdex-android.apk | sed 's# dist/# #' > dist/offdex-android.sha256
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  build_cli_upload:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, validate_cli, create_release]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: linux
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-x64-baseline
          - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            platform: linux
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-arm64
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: win32
            arch: x64
            ext: ".exe"
            target: bun-windows-x64-baseline
          - os: macos-15-intel
            platform: darwin
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-x64
          - os: macos-14
            platform: darwin
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-arm64
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          bun build --compile --target="${{ matrix.target }}" packages/bridge/src/cli.ts --outfile "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}"
          gzip -9 -c "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}" > "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}.gz"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          cd dist
          checksum_file="checksums-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}.txt"
          tmp_file="${checksum_file}.tmp"
          if command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              shasum -a 256 -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          else
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              sha256sum -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          fi
          mv "$tmp_file" "$checksum_file"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  publish_npm:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, build_cli_upload]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    env:
      NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          local_v="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          remote_v="$(npm view @dhruv2mars/offdex version 2>/dev/null || true)"
          if [ -n "${remote_v:-}" ] && [ "${remote_v}" = "${local_v}" ]; then
            echo "skip: already on npm (${remote_v})"
            exit 0
          fi
          rc="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/npmrc"
          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" > "$rc"
          export NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG="$rc"
          if [ -n "${NPM_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
            echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" >> "$rc"
            export NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="${NPM_TOKEN}"
            cd packages/npm && npm publish --access public
          else
            echo "publish via npm trusted publisher"
            unset NODE_AUTH_TOKEN || true
            if cd packages/npm && npm publish --provenance --access public; then
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "trusted publisher not configured and NPM_TOKEN missing"
            exit 1
          fi

  validate_web:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.web == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/web

  create_release:
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"
  repository_dispatch:
    types:

  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_tag:
        description: "Existing release tag to publish, ex: v0.0.1"
        required: true
        type: string
      publish_android:
        description: "Build and upload the signed Android APK"
        required: false
        default: false
        type: boolean

concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref_name }}

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  resolve_tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      publish_android: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.publish_android }}
      release_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.release_tag }}
    steps:
      - id: resolve
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          case "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" in
            repository_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.client_payload.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.client_payload.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            workflow_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.inputs.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.inputs.publish_android // false' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            *)
              release_tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
              publish_android="false"
              ;;
          esac
          [[ "${release_tag}" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]
          [[ "${publish_android}" == "true" || "${publish_android}" == "false" ]]
          echo "publish_android=${publish_android}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "release_tag=${release_tag}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }}
      version_code: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version_code }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: meta
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/package.json').version")"
          app_version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/app.json').expo.version")"
          npm_version="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          if [ "v${version}" != "${RELEASE_TAG}" ]; then
            echo "tag/version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${app_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "app.json/package.json mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${npm_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "npm/mobile version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          version_code="$(node -e "const [major, minor, patch] = process.argv[1].split('.').map(Number); process.stdout.write(String(major * 10000 + minor * 100 + patch));" "${version}")"
          echo "version=${version}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_code=${version_code}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  detect_areas:

    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cli: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.cli }}
      mobile: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.mobile }}
      web: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.web }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: areas
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: node scripts/release-areas.mjs "${RELEASE_TAG}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate_cli:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: npm test --prefix packages/npm
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/control-plane-worker/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/bridge
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/control-plane-worker
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test:install-smoke

  validate_mobile:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.mobile == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/mobile

  validate_web:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.web == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/web

  create_release:
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release view "${RELEASE_TAG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || gh release create "${RELEASE_TAG}" --title "${RELEASE_TAG}" --generate-notes

  build_android_release:
    if: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.publish_android == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, create_release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version }}
      RELEASE_VERSION_CODE: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version_code }}
      ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME: com.dhruv2mars.offdex
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 }}
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD }}
      ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS }}
      ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: 17
      - uses: android-actions/setup-android@v3
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for secret_name in ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD; do
            if [ -z "${!secret_name:-}" ]; then
              echo "Missing required secret: ${secret_name}"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          node - <<'NODE'
          const fs = require("node:fs");
          const path = "apps/mobile/app.json";
          const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path, "utf8"));
          config.expo.version = process.env.RELEASE_VERSION;
          config.expo.android = {
            ...config.expo.android,
            package: process.env.ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME,
            versionCode: Number(process.env.RELEASE_VERSION_CODE),
          };
          fs.writeFileSync(path, `${JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)}\n`);
          NODE
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing"
          printf '%s' "${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64}" | base64 --decode > "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore"
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile
        run: bunx expo prebuild --platform android --non-interactive --clean
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile/android
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.file="${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.password="${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.alias="${ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.password="${ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD}"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          cp apps/mobile/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk dist/offdex-android.apk
          shasum -a 256 dist/offdex-android.apk | sed 's# dist/# #' > dist/offdex-android.sha256
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

               ;;

<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

Use the `request_user_input` tool only when it is listed in the available tools for this turn.

In Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.
</collaboration_mode>

### How to use skills
- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.
- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.
- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.
- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):
  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.
  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.
  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.
  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.
  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.
- Coordination and sequencing:
  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.
  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.
- Context hygiene:
  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.
  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.
  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.
- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.
</skills_instructions>

Do not satisfy explicit @browser-use requests with macOS `open`, shell commands, Playwright, or generic web browsing unless the user approves a fallback.

- Preference: When a relevant plugin is available, prefer using capabilities associated with that plugin over standalone capabilities that provide similar functionality.
- Missing/blocked: If the user requests a plugin that is not listed above, or the plugin does not have relevant callable capabilities for the task, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.

# AGENTS.md instructions for /Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/offdex

<INSTRUCTIONS>
## Rules

- Define "done" before starting. Verify work before reporting. Fix failures before returning. Only stop when truly blocked.
- Use bun wherever possible. 
- Branch per task. PR to merge. Delete branch after merge. Small commits with `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:` prefixes.
- Always use relevant skills when possible. 
- For AFK tasks, spawn multiple subagents. For HITL tasks, work in the current main session.  


## Most Important Skills to Use:

- impeccable and emil-design skills for frontend design
- setup-matt-pocock-skills, grill-with-docs, to-prd, to-issues, tdd, diagnose, improve-codebase-architecture for development work
- full-output-enforcement for implementing tasks

--- project-doc ---

# Repository Guidelines

## Project Structure & Module Organization

Offdex is a Bun workspace managed with Turborepo. App code lives in `apps/`: `apps/mobile` is the Expo React Native client, and `apps/web` is the Next.js App Router site and web UI. Shared packages live in `packages/`: `protocol`, `bridge`, `relay`, `control-plane`, `control-plane-worker`, and the public npm wrapper in `packages/npm`. Tests sit beside each workspace in `test/` directories. Mobile assets are in `apps/mobile/assets`, native Expo module code is in `apps/mobile/modules`, and deployment helpers are in `deploy/`.

## Build, Test, and Development Commands

Use Bun unless a package script explicitly uses Node.

- `bun install`: install workspace dependencies and run local patch scripts.
- `bun run dev`: start all persistent dev tasks through Turbo.
- `bun run dev:web`, `bun run dev:mobile`, `bun run dev:bridge`: run one workspace.
- `bun run build`: run workspace builds and type-only package builds.
- `bun run test`: run all package and app tests.
- `bun run typecheck`: run TypeScript checks across the monorepo.
- `bun run lint`: run configured lint tasks.
- `bun run test:install-smoke`: smoke-test the npm installer package.

## Coding Style & Naming Conventions

Write TypeScript for app and package source. Follow existing formatting: two-space JSON indentation, lowercase kebab-case filenames such as `bridge-client.ts`, and PascalCase React components only where component exports require it. Prefer workspace imports like `@offdex/protocol` over relative cross-package paths. Keep environment-specific values behind documented `OFFDEX_*` variables.

## Testing Guidelines

Most workspaces use `bun test`; `packages/npm` uses Node's built-in test runner. Name tests `*.test.ts` or `*.test.js` and place them in the nearest `test/` directory. Add tests for protocol, pairing, CLI, installer, and routing behavior before changing implementation. After fixes, run the targeted workspace test and then `bun run test` when practical.

## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines

History uses short conventional commits, especially `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:`, and `chore:`. Keep commits atomic and scoped to one behavior. Pull requests should describe the change, list verification commands, link any related issue, and include screenshots or recordings for visible web or mobile UI changes.

## Security & Configuration Tips

Do not commit `.env*`, native build folders, `dist/`, `.next/`, or logs; these are ignored already. Treat release secrets, npm tokens, Android keystores, and relay URLs as private configuration. For local remote-pairing tests, prefer `OFFDEX_CONTROL_PLANE_URL` overrides instead of hardcoding endpoints.

## Agent skills

### Issue tracker

Issues and PRDs are tracked in GitHub Issues for this repo using the `gh` CLI. See `docs/agents/issue-tracker.md`.

### Triage labels

This repo uses the default engineering-skill triage labels. See `docs/agents/triage-labels.md`.

### Domain docs

This repo uses a single-context domain documentation layout. See `docs/agents/domain.md`.

## Rules

- Define "done" before starting. Verify work before reporting. Fix failures before returning. Only stop when truly blocked.
- Use bun wherever possible. 
- Branch per task. PR to merge. Delete branch after merge. Small commits with `feat:`, `fix:`, `test:` prefixes.
- Always use relevant skills when possible. 
- For AFK tasks, spawn multiple subagents. For HITL tasks, work in the current main session.  


## Most Important Skills to Use:

- impeccable and emil-design skills for frontend design
- setup-matt-pocock-skills, grill-with-docs, to-prd, to-issues, tdd, diagnose, improve-codebase-architecture for development work
- full-output-enforcement for implementing tasks

<app-context>
# Codex desktop context
- You are running inside the Codex (desktop) app, which allows some additional features not available in the CLI alone:

### Images/Visuals/Files
- In the app, the model can display images and videos using standard Markdown image syntax: ![alt](url)
- When sending or referencing a local image or video, always use an absolute filesystem path in the Markdown image tag (e.g., ![alt](/absolute/path.png)); relative paths and plain text will not render the media.
- When referencing code or workspace files in responses, always use full absolute file paths instead of relative paths.
- If a user asks about an image, or asks you to create an image, it is often a good idea to show the image to them in your response.
- Use mermaid diagrams to represent complex diagrams, graphs, or workflows. Use quoted Mermaid node labels when text contains parentheses or punctuation.
- Return web URLs as Markdown links (e.g., [label](https://example.com)).

### Workspace Dependencies
- For sheets, slides, and documents, call `load_workspace_dependencies` to find the bundled runtime and libraries.

### Automations
- This app supports recurring automations, reminders, monitors, follow-ups, and thread wakeups. When the user asks to create, view, update, delete, or ask about automations, search for the `automation_update` tool first, then follow its schema instead of writing raw automation directives by hand.

### Review findings
- Use the ::code-comment{...} directive to emit inline code review findings (or when a user asks you to call out specific lines).
- Emit one directive per finding; emit none when there are no findings.
- Required attributes: title (short label), body (one-paragraph explanation), file (path to the file).
- Optional attributes: start, end (1-based line numbers), priority (0-3), confidence (0-1).
- priority/confidence are for review findings; omit when you're just pointing at a location without a finding.
- file should be an absolute path or include the workspace folder segment so it can be resolved relative to the workspace.
- Keep line ranges tight; end defaults to start.
- Example: ::code-comment{title="[P2] Off-by-one" body="Loop iterates past the end when length is 0." file="/path/to/foo.ts" start=10 end=11 priority=2 confidence=0.55}

### Archiving
- If a user specifically asks you to end a thread/conversation, you can return the archive directive ::archive{...} to archive the thread/conversation.
- Example: ::archive{reason="User requested to end conversation"}

### Git
- After successfully staging files, emit `::git-stage{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a commit, emit `::git-commit{cwd="/absolute/path"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating or switching the thread onto a branch, emit `::git-create-branch{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully pushing the current branch, emit `::git-push{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name"}` on its own line in your final response.
- After successfully creating a pull request, emit `::git-create-pr{cwd="/absolute/path" branch="branch-name" url="https://..." isDraft=true}` on its own line in your final response. Include `isDraft=false` for ready PRs.
- Only emit these git directives in your final response after the action actually succeeds, never in commentary updates. Keep attributes single-line.
</app-context>

payload.aggregated_output:
name: release

on:
  push:
    tags:
      - "v*"
  repository_dispatch:
    types:
      - release-rerun
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      release_tag:
        description: "Existing release tag to publish, ex: v0.0.1"
        required: true
        type: string
      publish_android:
        description: "Build and upload the signed Android APK"
        required: false
        default: true
        type: boolean

concurrency: ${{ github.workflow }}-${{ github.ref_name }}

env:
  FORCE_JAVASCRIPT_ACTIONS_TO_NODE24: true

jobs:
  resolve_tag:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      publish_android: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.publish_android }}
      release_tag: ${{ steps.resolve.outputs.release_tag }}
    steps:
      - id: resolve
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          case "${GITHUB_EVENT_NAME}" in
            repository_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.client_payload.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.client_payload.publish_android // true' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            workflow_dispatch)
              release_tag="$(jq -r '.inputs.release_tag // empty' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              publish_android="$(jq -r '.inputs.publish_android // true' "$GITHUB_EVENT_PATH")"
              ;;
            *)
              release_tag="${GITHUB_REF_NAME}"
              publish_android="true"
              ;;
          esac
          [[ "${release_tag}" =~ ^v[0-9]+\.[0-9]+\.[0-9]+$ ]]
          [[ "${publish_android}" == "true" || "${publish_android}" == "false" ]]
          echo "publish_android=${publish_android}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "release_tag=${release_tag}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      version: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version }}
      version_code: ${{ steps.meta.outputs.version_code }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: meta
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/package.json').version")"
          app_version="$(node -p "require('./apps/mobile/app.json').expo.version")"
          npm_version="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          if [ "v${version}" != "${RELEASE_TAG}" ]; then
            echo "tag/version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${app_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "app.json/package.json mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          if [ "${npm_version}" != "${version}" ]; then
            echo "npm/mobile version mismatch"
            exit 1
          fi
          version_code="$(node -e "const [major, minor, patch] = process.argv[1].split('.').map(Number); process.stdout.write(String(major * 10000 + minor * 100 + patch));" "${version}")"
          echo "version=${version}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "version_code=${version_code}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  detect_areas:
    needs: resolve_tag
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      cli: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.cli }}
      mobile: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.mobile }}
      web: ${{ steps.areas.outputs.web }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          fetch-depth: 0
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - id: areas
        env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
        shell: bash
        run: node scripts/release-areas.mjs "${RELEASE_TAG}" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

  validate_cli:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: npm test --prefix packages/npm
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts packages/bridge/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun test packages/control-plane-worker/test/index.test.ts
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/bridge
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/control-plane-worker
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test:install-smoke

  validate_mobile:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.mobile == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/mobile

  validate_web:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.web == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/web

  create_release:
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release view "${RELEASE_TAG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || gh release create "${RELEASE_TAG}" --title "${RELEASE_TAG}" --generate-notes

  build_android_release:
    if: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.publish_android == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, create_release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version }}
      RELEASE_VERSION_CODE: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version_code }}
      ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME: com.dhruv2mars.offdex
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 }}
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD }}
      ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS }}
      ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: 17
      - uses: android-actions/setup-android@v3
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for secret_name in ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD; do
            if [ -z "${!secret_name:-}" ]; then
              echo "Missing required secret: ${secret_name}"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          node - <<'NODE'
          const fs = require("node:fs");
          const path = "apps/mobile/app.json";
          const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path, "utf8"));
          config.expo.version = process.env.RELEASE_VERSION;
          config.expo.android = {
            ...config.expo.android,
            package: process.env.ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME,
            versionCode: Number(process.env.RELEASE_VERSION_CODE),
          };
          fs.writeFileSync(path, `${JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)}\n`);
          NODE
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing"
          printf '%s' "${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64}" | base64 --decode > "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore"
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile
        run: bunx expo prebuild --platform android --non-interactive --clean
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile/android
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease \

      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/mobile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/mobile

  validate_web:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.web == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, detect_areas]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run test --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run typecheck --filter=@offdex/web
      - shell: bash
        run: bun run build --filter=@offdex/web

  create_release:
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - env:
          RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
          GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: gh release view "${RELEASE_TAG}" >/dev/null 2>&1 || gh release create "${RELEASE_TAG}" --title "${RELEASE_TAG}" --generate-notes

  build_android_release:
    if: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.publish_android == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, create_release]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      RELEASE_VERSION: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version }}
      RELEASE_VERSION_CODE: ${{ needs.validate.outputs.version_code }}
      ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME: com.dhruv2mars.offdex
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 }}
      ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD }}
      ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS }}
      ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD: ${{ secrets.ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
      - uses: actions/setup-java@v4
        with:
          distribution: temurin
          java-version: 17
      - uses: android-actions/setup-android@v3
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for secret_name in ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64 ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD; do
            if [ -z "${!secret_name:-}" ]; then
              echo "Missing required secret: ${secret_name}"
              exit 1
            fi
          done
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          node - <<'NODE'
          const fs = require("node:fs");
          const path = "apps/mobile/app.json";
          const config = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(path, "utf8"));
          config.expo.version = process.env.RELEASE_VERSION;
          config.expo.android = {
            ...config.expo.android,
            package: process.env.ANDROID_PACKAGE_NAME,
            versionCode: Number(process.env.RELEASE_VERSION_CODE),
          };
          fs.writeFileSync(path, `${JSON.stringify(config, null, 2)}\n`);
          NODE
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing"
          printf '%s' "${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64}" | base64 --decode > "${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore"
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile
        run: bunx expo prebuild --platform android --non-interactive --clean
      - shell: bash
        working-directory: apps/mobile/android
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          ./gradlew :app:assembleRelease \

payload.aggregated_output:
./gradlew :app:assembleRelease \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.file="${RUNNER_TEMP}/android-signing/offdex-release.keystore" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.store.password="${ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.alias="${ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS}" \
            -Pandroid.injected.signing.key.password="${ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD}"
      - shell: bash

          mkdir -p dist
          cp apps/mobile/android/app/build/outputs/apk/release/app-release.apk dist/offdex-android.apk
          shasum -a 256 dist/offdex-android.apk | sed 's# dist/# #' > dist/offdex-android.sha256
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  build_cli_upload:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, validate_cli, create_release]
    strategy:
      fail-fast: false
      matrix:
        include:
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: linux
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-x64-baseline
          - os: ubuntu-24.04-arm
            platform: linux
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-linux-arm64
          - os: ubuntu-latest
            platform: win32
            arch: x64
            ext: ".exe"
            target: bun-windows-x64-baseline
          - os: macos-15-intel
            platform: darwin
            arch: x64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-x64
          - os: macos-14
            platform: darwin
            arch: arm64
            ext: ""
            target: bun-darwin-arm64
    runs-on: ${{ matrix.os }}
    permissions:
      contents: write
    env:
      RELEASE_TAG: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      GH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: oven-sh/setup-bun@v2
      - shell: bash
        run: bun install --frozen-lockfile
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          mkdir -p dist
          bun build --compile --target="${{ matrix.target }}" packages/bridge/src/cli.ts --outfile "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}"
          gzip -9 -c "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}" > "dist/offdex-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.ext }}.gz"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          cd dist
          checksum_file="checksums-${{ matrix.platform }}-${{ matrix.arch }}.txt"
          tmp_file="${checksum_file}.tmp"
          if command -v shasum >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              shasum -a 256 -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          else
            for f in ./offdex-*; do
              [ -f "$f" ] || continue
              sha256sum -- "$f"
            done | sed 's# \./# #' > "$tmp_file"
          fi
          mv "$tmp_file" "$checksum_file"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          for file in dist/*; do
            gh release upload --clobber "${RELEASE_TAG}" "${file}"
          done

  publish_npm:
    if: ${{ needs.detect_areas.outputs.cli == 'true' }}
    needs: [resolve_tag, validate, detect_areas, build_cli_upload]
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    permissions:
      contents: read
      id-token: write
    env:
      NPM_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v5
        with:
          ref: ${{ needs.resolve_tag.outputs.release_tag }}
      - uses: actions/setup-node@v5
        with:
          node-version: 24
          registry-url: "https://registry.npmjs.org"
      - shell: bash
        run: |
          set -euo pipefail
          local_v="$(node -p "require('./packages/npm/package.json').version")"
          remote_v="$(npm view @dhruv2mars/offdex version 2>/dev/null || true)"
          if [ -n "${remote_v:-}" ] && [ "${remote_v}" = "${local_v}" ]; then
            echo "skip: already on npm (${remote_v})"
            exit 0
          fi
          rc="${RUNNER_TEMP:-/tmp}/npmrc"
          echo "registry=https://registry.npmjs.org/" > "$rc"
          export NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG="$rc"
          if [ -n "${NPM_TOKEN:-}" ]; then
            echo "//registry.npmjs.org/:_authToken=${NPM_TOKEN}" >> "$rc"
            export NODE_AUTH_TOKEN="${NPM_TOKEN}"
            cd packages/npm && npm publish --access public
          else
            echo "publish via npm trusted publisher"
            unset NODE_AUTH_TOKEN || true
            if cd packages/npm && npm publish --provenance --access public; then
              exit 0
            fi
            echo "trusted publisher not configured and NPM_TOKEN missing"
            exit 1
          fi

payload.arguments:
{"cmd":"cat > /tmp/offdex-pr-body.md <<'EOF'
## Summary
- add the WebUI connection URL to `offdex status`
- add pairing guidance to running status output
- add the bridge log path to offline status output
- sync `bun.lock` package versions after `bun install`
- make public tag releases build and upload `offdex-android.apk` by default
- add Android signing preflight checks so bad release secrets fail before the long Gradle build
- clean up web lint issues found during the readiness pass

## Verification
- `bun test packages/bridge/test/cli-lib.test.ts`
- `bun test packages/bridge/test`
- `node --test packages/npm/test/offdex-lib.test.js`
- `node --test packages/npm/test/release-contract.test.js`
- `bun run test:install-smoke`
- `bun run test`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run build`
- `bun run lint`

## Release readiness note
The latest public release still does not have `offdex-android.apk` because the GitHub Android signing secrets failed during packaging with a keystore/key password error. This PR fixes the release contract and catches that secret mismatch early; publishing the APK still requires replacing the repository Android signing secrets with a matching keystore, alias, store password, and key password, then rerunning the release workflow for `v0.0.13`.

Refs #73
EOF

## Summary

- sync `bun.lock` package versions after `bun install`
- make public tag releases build and upload `offdex-android.apk` by default

Work done in PR: [#74](https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/pull/74)

Changed:
- `offdex status` now shows WebUI URL, pairing hint, and log path.
- Release workflow now builds/uploads `offdex-android.apk` by default for public tags.
- Release workflow now validates Android keystore/password/alias/key password before long Gradle build.
- Web lint issues fixed.

Verified:
- `bun run test:install-smoke`
- `bun run test`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run build`
- `bun run lint`
- release contract test passed

Hard blocker remains: latest release `v0.0.13` still has no `offdex-android.apk`. GitHub release build failed because Android signing secrets are bad/mismatched:

`ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64`, `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD`, `ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS`, `ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD`

Need fix those repo secrets, then rerun release workflow for `v0.0.13`. Until that, user can install CLI, but cannot download public mobile APK from release page. I did not fake this with debug APK because public users need proper signed app.

Untouched local user files still dirty: `AGENTS.md`, `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/*`.

content.text:
Work done in PR: [#74](https://github.com/Dhruv2mars/offdex/pull/74)

Changed:
- `offdex status` now shows WebUI URL, pairing hint, and log path.
- Release workflow now builds/uploads `offdex-android.apk` by default for public tags.
- Release workflow now validates Android keystore/password/alias/key password before long Gradle build.
- Web lint issues fixed.

Verified:
- `bun run test:install-smoke`
- `bun run test`
- `bun run typecheck`
- `bun run build`
- `bun run lint`
- release contract test passed

Hard blocker remains: latest release `v0.0.13` still has no `offdex-android.apk`. GitHub release build failed because Android signing secrets are bad/mismatched:

`ANDROID_KEYSTORE_BASE64`, `ANDROID_KEYSTORE_PASSWORD`, `ANDROID_KEY_ALIAS`, `ANDROID_KEY_PASSWORD`

Need fix those repo secrets, then rerun release workflow for `v0.0.13`. Until that, user can install CLI, but cannot download public mobile APK from release page. I did not fake this with debug APK because public users need proper signed app.

Untouched local user files still dirty: `AGENTS.md`, `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/*`.

::git-commit{cwd="/Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/offdex"}
::git-push{cwd="/Users/dhruv2mars/dev/github/offdex" branch="fix/public-cli-status-diagnostics"}
